usage: create_bump [-help] [-allow_outside_of_die] [-allow_overlap_control {keep_all keep_existing_bumps keep_new_bumps}] -cell <bumpcell_name> [-check_drc] [-edge_spacing <left bottom right top>] [-exclude_bump_area {rect_list}] [-loc <x y>] [-loc_type {cell_center cell_lowerleft geometry_center geometry_lowerleft}] [-name_format {string, %i, %r, %c}] [-orientation {R0 R90 R180 R270 MX MX90 MY MY90}] [-respect_placement_blockage] [-return_bumps_name] [-start_index <index>] [-pitch <x y> [-pattern_full_chip  | -pattern_side {side width} | -pattern_array {row colunm} | -pattern_ring <width> | -pattern_center {row column}]] [-stagger_type <type> [-stagger_offset <value>]] [ [-keep_out_region <width height>] | [-keep_out_pitch <value>] ] [-relative_type {embedded_bump inst_pin_port block} [[-relative_object {inst_pin_port_list}] [-relative_block <inst+>] [-relative_block_constraint <fileName>] [-relative_assignment ] [-relative_offset <x y>]]] [{-pattern_full_chip  | -pattern_array {row colunm} | -pattern_center {row column}} [-start_from <type>]]

options.name: -help
options.description: Prints out the command usage

options.name: -allow_outside_of_die
options.description: Allow bumps to be created outside of die area.

options.name: -allow_overlap_control
options.description: {keep_all keep_existing_bumps keep_new_bumps} It specifies the overlap control type.

options.name: -cell
options.description: It specifies the name of bump cell, which is used to create bumps.

options.name: -check_drc
options.description: It checks drc rules and does not create bump at the spot where exists violation.

options.name: -edge_spacing
options.description: It specifies the minimum distances in micron to the edge of the chip from the lower left or center of the outermost bumps. The -loc_type value determines which point of the bump (lower-left or center of the cell or geometry) is used to compute the distance to the edge. The arrays start at the left, bottom location, and extend to the right and top.

options.name: -exclude_bump_area
options.description: It specifies the areas not to allow bump placement.

options.name: -keep_out_pitch
options.description: It specifies a pitch with bump center as the center, where bump is not going to be created, if there are existing bumps violating center-to-center pitch.

options.name: -keep_out_region
options.description: It specifies a region with width and height with bump center as the center, where bump is not going to be created, if there are existing bumps.

options.name: -loc
options.description: It specifies the x and y coordinates of the start point for bump creation based on location type.

options.name: -loc_type
options.description: {cell_center cell_lowerleft geometry_center geometry_lowerleft} It specifies the location type for bump creation.

options.name: -name_format
options.description: It specifies the format of bump name like a printf format except it uses %i for index, %r for row number and %c for column number. By default the format is 'Bump_%i'.

options.name: -orientation
options.description: {R0 R90 R180 R270 MX MX90 MY MY90} It specifies the orientation of bump for bump generation.

options.name: -pattern_array
options.description: It creates an array of bumps. The bump starts at the coordinates specified by -loc, and extends to the number of <row> and <column> values given from the start corner specified by -start_from.

options.name: -pattern_center
options.description: It creates an array of bumps that is centered on the center of chip and that extends for the <row> and <column> values given. The center point can optionally be set directly with -loc. The start corner of array can be specified by -start_from.

options.name: -pattern_full_chip
options.description: It creates an array of bumps covering the chip. The array starts at the most closest to boundary point set by -edge_spacing at the corner specified by -start_from, and extends until within -edge_spacing of the chip boundary for the opposite sides. You can optionally directly specify the -loc value for the start point, in which case -edge_spacing only affects the two opposite edges of the array.

options.name: -pattern_ring
options.description: It creates a ring of bumps around the boundary of the chip that is <width> bumps wide. The ring extends until within -edge_spacing of the chip boundary, starting at the lower-left value allowed by -edge_spacing. You can optionally directly specify the -loc value for the lower-left bump location, in which case -edge_spacing only affects the right and top edges of the array. The -pitch x value is the spacing between bumps inside one ring, and the -pitch y value is the spacing between one ring to the next ring.

options.name: -pattern_side
options.description: It creates an array of bumps along one side of the chip. The array is the length of the side, and <width> bumps wide. The legal <side> names are: left, top, right, bottom. <Width> is integer. The array starts at the left/bottom controlled by -edge_spacing, and extends until within -edge_spacing of the top/right sides. You can optionally directly specify the -loc value for the lower-left bump location for bottom/left sides, the upper-left bump for the top side, and lower-right for the right side.

options.name: -pitch
options.description: It specifies the distance between bump centers in microns.

options.name: -relative_assignment
options.description: It assigns the corresponding net of relative object to bump.

options.name: -relative_block
options.description: It specifies the block list. ((inst)+, optional)

options.name: -relative_block_constraint
options.description: It specifies the file name of block constraint.

options.name: -relative_object
options.description: It specifies the inst pin port list. Format is {inst:pin:port ...} and port is int type.

options.name: -relative_offset
options.description: It specifies the offset of bump location compared with the relative object.

options.name: -relative_type
options.description: {embedded_bump inst_pin_port block} It specifies the type of relative object.

options.name: -respect_placement_blockage
options.description: It does not create bump overlapping with place blockage.

options.name: -return_bumps_name
options.description: It returns names of generated bumps.

options.name: -stagger_offset
options.description: It specifies the offset between different rows or columns in microns.

options.name: -stagger_type
options.description: {odd_rows odd_columns even_rows even_columns trapezoid} It specifies the stagger type.

options.name: -start_from
options.description: {lower_left upper_left lower_right upper_right} It specifies the starting corner for an array of bumps creation.

options.name: -start_index
options.description: It specifies the starting number of the index for bumps to be created.
